title: Mesosphaerum latifolium
taxonomy: Mesosphaerum latifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Mesosphaerum, which is a group of plants that are commonly known as "bee balm" or "wild basil". The Lamiaceae family, also known as the mint family, is a large and diverse group of plants that are commonly found in temperate and tropical regions. Mesosphaerum latifolium is native to South America, where it can be found growing in a variety of habitats, from tropical forests to grasslands. The plant's classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower structure, which are typical of the Lamiaceae family.

intro: Mesosphaerum latifolium, also known as Brazilian peppertree or Brazilian rosemary, is a small shrub native to South America.
